title: campanula macrorhiza var. bertolae
intro: Campanula macrorhiza var. bertolae, also known as Bertolucci's bellflower, is a variety of the Campanula macrorhiza plant, a perennial plant native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe and heal wounds, reduce swelling, and fight off infections. The health benefits of Bertolucci's bellflower include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote overall well-being. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion, or dried and made into a powder for use in herbal supplements and remedies.
